list.help: List the current authenticated Easyship account as a single-row collection.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of accounts to return

list.usage_instructions: Use `easyship account list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get the current Easyship account payload.
get.usage_instructions: Use `easyship account get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

account.usage_instructions: Use `easyship account` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of couriers to return

list.usage_instructions: Use `easyship couriers list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.usage_instructions: Use `easyship couriers get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

couriers.usage_instructions: Use `easyship couriers` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth login` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth logout` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth status` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth test` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth profiles list` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth profiles get` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth profiles create` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth profiles set-default` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth profiles delete` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth profiles` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `easyship auth` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Use `easyship cache clear` with the documented arguments and options shown below.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `easyship cache` to access this command group. Run `--help` on a subcommand for details.

usage_instructions: Use `` with the documented arguments and options shown below.
